subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **64 cards** — 8 types of critters, 8 cards of each:
  - Cockroach, Bat, Fly, Toad, Rat, Scorpion, Spider, Stink Bug

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Shuffle all 64 cards.**
2. **Deal entire deck** evenly to all players.
3. **No market or discard** — Cards are only in hands or placed face-up in front of players.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Hold your cards** — Keep your hand hidden.
- **No table cards** to start — Cards accumulate in front of you when you're wrong.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Cockroach Poker is a pure bluffing game. Push cards to other players claiming (truthfully or not) what they are. If the recipient guesses wrong, they keep the card face up. Collect 4 of the same type and you lose!

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Offer** — Choose a card from your hand, place it face down in front of another player, and declare it as a type ("This is a cockroach" — may be true or a bluff).
2. **Recipient decides** — They either:
   - **Accept the claim** — "Yes, it's a cockroach" or "No, it's not a cockroach."
   - **Pass it on** — Peek at the card, then pass it face down to another player with a new (or same) claim.
3. **Resolve** — Flip the card:
   - If the recipient was **right**, the offerer keeps the card face up.
   - If the recipient was **wrong**, the recipient keeps the card face up.
4. **Loser starts next round** — Whoever received the face-up card offers next.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Bluffing
- You can claim any type regardless of the actual card.
- "This is a rat" could be a rat or could be any other critter.

#### Passing Along
- Instead of guessing, peek at the card and pass it to someone else.
- You may change the claim or keep it.
- Cannot pass back to someone who already saw the card this round.

#### Collecting Cards
- Face-up cards in front of you are public knowledge.
- 4 of the same type = you lose.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **All must be offered** — Cannot pass the card to someone who has already seen it this round.
- **No refusal** — If the card comes to you and everyone else has seen it, you must guess.
- **No elimination** until loss condition.
- **Empty hand** — If you run out of cards in hand and have to offer, you lose.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Losing Condition
- A player with **4 face-up cards of the same type** loses immediately. Everyone else wins.
- A player who must offer but has **no cards in hand** also loses.

#### No Scoring
- This is a loss-avoidance game. There is no points system.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Tell the truth sometimes** — If you always lie, you're predictable.
- **Target the vulnerable** — Offer cards of types a player already has 3 of face up.
- **Pass strategically** — Peeking lets you make a more informed pass.
- **Watch faces** — Body language gives away confidence levels.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Reverse psychology** — Claim the actual card type when opponents expect a bluff.
- **Threat targeting** — Funnel specific types to one player to force a loss.
- **Deflection** — When passing, change the claim to something the next player already has.
- **Reading patterns** — Note if a player tends to lie or tell truth and exploit their tendencies.
